methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional capacity-building in Richmond Metro, but the university-centric model risks deepening metropolitan concentration rather than distributing AI fluency to surrounding regions without higher ed anchors.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's ed2go platform partnership enables on-demand workforce training outside major metros, but reliance on third-party platforms suggests shallow, consumption-oriented adoption rather than deep capability development in underserved regions.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding skill-building infrastructure signals displacement risk—workers feel the impact of AI transformation but lack pathways to deep capability, creating anxiety rather than empowerment.

signals.title: Roanoke College president: Virginia’s future workforce depends on fixing the path from college to career - Cardinal News
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College president's urgent call to fix college-to-career pathways reveals a structural gap in workforce preparation that AI acceleration will exacerbate—unless addressed, Southwest Virginia risks neither depth nor distribution of AI capability.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: Real estate agent adoption of AI tools in Hampton Roads demonstrates platform-driven shallow distribution—professionals use AI to enhance existing work but aren't developing deep technical capabilities, fitting the 'software does it for you' pattern in a non-metro-hub region.
